drifts.title: Public model selection differs from local rerun metric leader
drifts.canonical_side: Public operational truth preserves weighted XGBoost at threshold 0.29.
drifts.current_side: Current checked-in comparison artifact records rf_balanced as the local rerun best-cost leader at threshold 0.27, while metadata notes still preserve weighted XGBoost at 0.29 as the selected public model.
drifts.user_visible_risk: Users or future assistants may incorrectly treat the rerun leader table as replacing the published public model story.
drifts.upgrade_handling_rule: Preserve the public reference selection as canonical until an explicit governed upgrade intentionally changes it and updates both metadata and narrative sources together.
notes: This drift is explicit rather than hidden, but it still needs tracking.

drifts.title: Fallback runtime row logic differs from artifact-backed row truth
drifts.canonical_side: Artifact-backed row-level truth uses employee_scores.parquet with attrition_probability and selected_threshold_flag.
drifts.current_side: Fallback mode derives screening_score_v1, high_risk_flag_v1, and risk_cost_exposure_index_v1 from heuristic feature weighting inside load_data.py.
drifts.user_visible_risk: If labels blur, a fallback exploratory score could be mistaken for the final model probability.
drifts.upgrade_handling_rule: Keep fallback mode explicitly labeled and do not merge its fields into canonical public model truth.
notes: This is a designed separation, but it is still a meaningful truth-layer drift.

drifts.title: Static figure layer can diverge from generated artifact layer
drifts.canonical_side: Generated tables and metadata are the current structured runtime-consumed artifact truth when available.
drifts.current_side: Several pages still rely on static PNG figures as stable reference or fallback presentation assets without explicit version linkage to the current checked-in artifact snapshot.
drifts.user_visible_risk: Presentation visuals may lag or differ from the latest structured artifact snapshot without an obvious provenance cue.
drifts.upgrade_handling_rule: Treat static figures as presentation/reference assets unless provenance is explicitly tied to the current artifact snapshot.
notes: This is directly observable from code paths even though the visual content itself was only metadata-inspected.

drifts.title: Builder references a trusted sibling workflow but locally reimplements model logic
drifts.canonical_side: Builder and metadata language say the offline build is sourced from the trusted sibling modeling workflow.
drifts.current_side: The builder performs local feature engineering, model fitting, threshold search, and SHAP generation in this repo rather than importing or executing the sibling script directly.
drifts.user_visible_risk: Future maintainers may overestimate direct reproducibility against the original workflow and miss local behavioral drift.
drifts.upgrade_handling_rule: Document this as governed divergence or refactor later to a more explicit provenance boundary; do not assume the sibling script is being executed today.
notes: This is one of the most important governance drifts in the repo.

drifts.title: Optional model_modes_summary contract does not match the current file shape
drifts.canonical_side: artifact_contract.json declares flattened required fields for model_modes_summary.json.
drifts.current_side: The checked-in model_modes_summary.json and builder output use nested keys like operational, survey_rich, comparison_notes, and source_workflow instead of the flattened contract fields.
drifts.user_visible_risk: Schema validation or future indexing could fail or encode the file incorrectly.
drifts.upgrade_handling_rule: Choose one source of truth for this file shape before using it in downstream automation or navigator loaders.
notes: Directly verified from current repo contents.

drifts.title: employee_scores artifact includes extra columns beyond the declared required contract
drifts.canonical_side: artifact_contract.json defines a required employee_scores column set and a small optional set.
drifts.current_side: Current employee_scores.parquet also contains satisfaction_level, attrition_label, high_risk_flag, and employee_id, which are not declared in the current contract.
drifts.user_visible_risk: Downstream consumers may make assumptions based on undeclared fields or misread them as guaranteed long-term contract elements.
drifts.upgrade_handling_rule: Treat undeclared extra columns as convenience fields until the contract is formally updated.
notes: This drift is low severity because runtime does not currently break on the extra columns.

drifts.title: Public canonical narrative and current snapshot evidence need explicit separation
drifts.canonical_side: Public app copy presents a polished operational story centered on weighted XGBoost, threshold choice, department exposure, and SHAP explainability.
drifts.current_side: Current metadata snapshot includes provenance notes and local rerun evidence that are useful for auditability but not always surfaced in the public-facing page copy.
drifts.user_visible_risk: Future upgrades may either over-audit public copy or over-polish runtime truth and erase the important distinction between narrative and snapshot evidence.
drifts.upgrade_handling_rule: Keep public story, runtime truth, and audit/provenance evidence in separate layers with explicit cross-links.
notes: This is partially confirmed because the repo contains both layers, but not every boundary is formalized yet.
